title: Concurrency, Shared State, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Load Shedding · Execution Trace: Load Shed Priority
summary: Concurrent handlers race on a map, launch unbounded goroutines, queue work after clients leave, share one global rate limit, and accept more expensive work than dependencies can sustain. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo load shed priority increment in concurrency budget and overload decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject before expensive work when queue, dependency, memory, deadline, or priority budgets are exhausted and preserve recovery capacity.
Choose trusted identity, token bucket or window semantics, burst, refill, distributed consistency, Retry-After, and bypass policy from abuse risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Load Shed Priority · Theory Load Shed Priority
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7928 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine concurrency capacity with a separate tenant rate budget; then lower the byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at the owning boundary. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Load Shed Priority contract for the supplied data.
